messages.content:
You are comparing two analyst responses to determine which better demonstrates knowledge of a specific finding from a longitudinal dataset. Question asked: Based on the SIEM data so far, are there any users exhibiting unusual access patterns? Finding to evaluate: data transfer volumes from a single account diverge from peer group norms in a sustained multi-week pattern Response A: **Yes – several users stand out as exhibiting atypical access behavior in the SIEM logs collected so far.** | User | Why the activity is unusual | Supporting evidence | |------|----------------------------|----------------------| | **jmorris** | • Accessed an unusually large number of repositories (9 different repos) in a single day and downloaded the highest number of files (49) with a total volume of ≈ 232 MB – far above the typical 30‑40 files/≈120 MB seen for most users.
• Triggered multiple DLP alerts on the same day (two FILE‑SIZE‑THRESHOLD alerts on PDFs).
• Held a long VPN session (2.4 h) that extended past normal working hours. | 2024‑09‑11 Access Log shows 9 repos and 49 files (127 MB‑231 MB) 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】; DLP alerts for jmorris (PDF size thresholds) 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】; VPN session of 2.4 h for jmorris 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】 | | **sbrown** | • Consistently appears in the “high‑volume” tier (e.g., 36‑40 files per day, > 80 MB) and also accessed many repos (up to 5 in a single day).
• Generated an EXTERNAL‑TRANSFER DLP alert (email attachment of a .dwg file).
• Had the longest VPN session on 9/11 (2.4 h) and a second‑day VPN session lasting > 1 h on 9/09. | 2024‑09‑11 Access Log shows 36 files, 87 MB and multiple repos 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】; DLP alert for sbrown (EXTERNAL‑TRANSFER) 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】; VPN session 2.4 h for sbrown 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】 | | **dlee** | • Frequently hits bulk‑download DLP rules (e.g., on 9/06 and 9/11) indicating large‑scale data extraction.
• Was blocked from using a USB STORAGE device on 9/11, a rare event for most users.
• Shows after‑hours VPN usage (multiple sessions spanning 19:00‑20:00) and a 2‑hour VPN session on 9/13. | Bulk‑download DLP alerts for dlee on 9/06 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_005】 and 9/11 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】; USB STORAGE blocked event on 9/11 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】; VPN sessions of 1‑2 h after normal hours (e.g., 19:29‑20:17 on 9/06) 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_005】 | | **hkim** (secondary) | • Repeated USB‑PRINTER and USB‑HID blocks across several days, suggesting attempts to use prohibited peripherals.
• Occasionally shows longer VPN sessions (≈1 h) and accesses multiple repos in a short window. | USB‑PRINTER blocked on 9/09 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_008】; USB‑HID blocked on 9/06 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_005】; VPN session 1.1 h on 9/09 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_008】 | **Interpretation** - **High‑volume data pulls** (jmorris, sbrown, dlee) and **multiple repository accesses** in a single day exceed the baseline pattern (most users access 2‑4 repos and download ≤ 40 files/≈120 MB). - **Repeated DLP alerts** (especially bulk‑download or external‑transfer policies) indicate potential data‑exfiltration attempts. - **After‑hours VPN sessions** and **blocked USB device usage** are additional red flags that deviate from normal employee behavior. These users should be prioritized for deeper investigation (e.g., contextual user interviews, review of the specific files accessed, and verification of legitimate business need). Response B: **Yes – several individuals stand out for repeatedly deviating from normal‑day activity.** | User | Why the activity is unusual (chronology) | Log IDs | |------|------------------------------------------|---------| | **sbrown** | • **Very high download volume** – 40 files (169 MB) on 9‑02, 27 files (96 MB) on 9‑03, 34 files (140 MB) on 9‑05, 27 files (96 MB) on 9‑06 and 8 files (120 MB) on 9‑06 EP.
• **Multiple DLP alerts** – FILE_SIZE_THRESHOLD on 9‑02 (low) and 9‑04 (low ENCRYPTED_ARCHIVE_DETECT) and EXTERNAL_TRANSFER_POLICY on 9‑05 (medium) and SENSITIVE_KEYWORD_MATCH on 9‑02 (low).
• **Blocked USB storage device** on 9‑02, 9‑04 (twice) and 9‑06 (allowed once after two blocks).
• **Late‑night VPN usage** – 19:41‑21:29 (1.8 h) on 9‑03 and 17:26‑18:26 (1 h) on 9‑05.
• **Badge‑swipe anomalies** – Lab B at 07:48 (weekday) then Main Entrance at 07:44 on 9‑05, plus a Cafeteria swipe at 08:04 on 9‑06. | [insider_threat_05_ep_001] – access & DLP, [insider_threat_05_ep_001] – USB, [insider_threat_05_ep_001] – badge, [insider_threat_05_ep_002] – VPN, [insider_threat_05_ep_004] – DLP, [insider_threat_05_ep_005] – DLP & USB, [insider_threat_05_ep_006] – badge | | **kwilson** | • **Bulk‑download and external‑transfer alerts** – BULK_DOWNLOAD_POLICY (MEDIUM) on 9‑03, EXTERNAL_TRANSFER_POLICY (LOW) on 9‑03, FILE_SIZE_THRESHOLD (MEDIUM) on 9‑06.
• **Large download bursts** – 13 files (112 MB) on 9‑02, 39 files (160 MB) on 9‑05, 39 files (160 MB) on 9‑05 EP.
• **VPN sessions in the evening** – 19:58‑20:46 (0.8 h) on 9‑03 and 19:58‑20:46 (0.8 h) on 9‑05.
• **Badge‑swipe at Main Entrance (07:29) and Lab A (07:44) on 9‑05, then Lab A again on 9‑06. | [insider_threat_05_ep_001] – access & DLP, [insider_threat_05_ep_003] – DLP, [insider_th Which response better demonstrates awareness of this finding? The response may use different terminology. Focus on whether the core insight is present, not exact wording. Respond with ONLY one word: A, B, or TIE /no_think
